title: The Honest Woodcutter
content:
A woodcutter worked hard every day near a deep, cold river, chopping wood to sell at the market. He was an honest man who never told a lie. One afternoon, his heavy iron axe slipped from his hands and fell into the river with a splash!

The river was too cold and deep to wade into. The woodcutter sat down on the bank and put his face in his hands. Without his axe, he could not work. Without work, he could not feed his family. He felt very sad.

A river spirit rose from the water. "Why are you crying?" she asked gently. The woodcutter explained. The spirit dove in and came back with a gleaming golden axe. "Is this yours?" she asked. "No," said the woodcutter firmly. "That is not mine."

The spirit dove again and rose with a beautiful silver axe. "Is this yours?" "No, that is not mine either," said the woodcutter. The spirit dove once more and brought up a plain iron axe. "That is mine!" the woodcutter cried joyfully.

The spirit smiled warmly. "Your honesty has pleased me greatly." She gave him all three axes, the golden, the silver, and the plain iron one. The woodcutter went home that evening richer than he had ever been. Honesty, he knew, was always the best choice.

author: Aesop
